data.title: Bitmine Immersion Technologies reports $11.8B crypto + cash treasury, 4.875M ETH (4.04%) and 3.33M ETH staked
data.description:
Bitmine Immersion Technologies announced $11.8 billion in total crypto, cash and "moonshots" holdings, including 4.875 million ETH and $719 million cash.

Key Highlights:

  • Holds 4,874,858 ETH (4.04% of 120.7M supply) valued at $2,206 per ETH.
  • Total crypto + cash + moonshots equal $11.8 billion, including $719M cash and $85M in ORBS.
  • 3,334,637 ETH are staked (~68% of its ETH), representing $7.4B and generating $212M annualized staking revenue.
  • Launched MAVAN institutional staking platform; portion of ETH already staked on MAVAN to serve institutions.
  • Uplisted to the New York Stock Exchange effective April 9, 2026; stock trades under symbol BMNR.

Original SEC Filing: BITMINE IMMERSION TECHNOLOGIES, INC. [ BMNR ] - 8-K - Apr. 13, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: India's ICICI Prudential AMC quarterly profit jumps on strong domestic inflows
data.description:
India's ICICI Prudential Asset Management Company reported a 10.4% rise in fourth-quarter profit on Monday, helped by robust domestic inflows as investors used the market correction as an opportunity to increase investments.

  • The country's most valuable asset manager by market capitalisation posted a profit of 7.63 billion rupees ($81.76 million) for the quarter ended March 31, up from 6.92 billion rupees a year earlier.

  • Revenue from operations grew 19.5% to 15.17 billion rupees.

  • Strong inflows into equity mutual funds during the quarter, driven largely by retail investors, helped cushion the market from persistent foreign outflows and supported the asset management industry.

  • Inflows into Indian equity mutual funds rose 56% to an eight-month high in March, data from the Association of Mutual Funds in India showed, even as foreign portfolio investors sold record amounts of Indian shares driven by elevated crude prices and Iran war concerns.

  • ICICI Prudential AMC's quarterly average assets under management for mutual funds grew 24.9%, led by contributions from equity funds.

  • The firm, a joint venture between India's second-largest private lender ICICI Bank and British insurer Prudential , is up about 30% since its trading debut on December 19.

  • The AMC also declared a dividend of 12.4 rupees per share.

  • Indian equities fell sharply during the quarter, with the benchmark Nifty 50 declining about 14.5%, weighed down by rising oil prices and concerns over inflation and economic growth as geopolitical tensions intensified.

($1 = 93.3270 Indian rupees)

data.title: AGS WEEK AHEAD: Grains Nudge Higher Amid Oil Disruption
data.description:
By Joe Stonor

A roundup of key agricultural commodity markets for the week of April 13-17 by Dow Jones Newswires in Barcelona.

GRAINS & OILSEEDS:

Investors pulled back from agricultural commodities last week as a cease-fire agreement between the U.S. and Iran sparked a fall in oil prices.

However, the stalemate in peace talks between the two sides over the weekend sent oil higher Monday, a bullish signal for broader commodity prices. Agricultural traders will track developments in peace talks closely, with any escalation in hostilities likely leading to a war premium.

More broadly, agricultural commodities are pulled between climbing oil prices and an investor pullback from risk, analysts at Peak Trading Research wrote in a note.

Hedge funds pivoted hard against agricultural commodities in the week to April 7, ending an eight-week run of net buying, according to the Commodity Futures Trading Commission's weekly Commitments of Traders report released on Friday.

Institutional money exited agricultural commodities in its largest volumes since January.

However, higher energy prices in the long term, as well as dry weather across U.S. plains and hopes around a U.S.-China trade deal, remain bullish signals for agricultural commodities, Peak Trading Research analysts said.

Data on U.S. planting progress--the first of the season for soybeans--is set for release later Monday.

Wet weather is delaying harvest in Argentina slightly, while rains in the U.S. corn belt are delaying some farm work in the short-term, the analysts said.

However, drought across the U.S. Great Plains--particularly in central and western Kansas--could drag on wheat production.

Wheat futures rose 2.2% in Chicago to $5.84 a bushel in European afternoon trade Monday, largely in line with pre-war prices for the commodity. Corn futures edged up 0.85% to $4.45 a bushel, Soybean was 0.15% lower at $11.74 a bushel.

SOFT COMMODITIES:

Coffee prices fell sharply in New York Monday. Coffee beans dropped in afternoon European trade by 1.45% to $2.96 a pound as expectations of oversupply continued to weigh on the commodity.

Cocoa edged down 0.5% to $3,311 a metric ton, though prices remained elevated above pre-war levels. Cocoa prices fell last week as supply gluts outweighed ongoing weather concerns, Rabobank analysts said.

The European Cocoa Association is set to publish grind data--a key measure of demand across the continent--on Thursday.

Sugar prices have fallen sharply in April from highs reached in March, when surging oil prices threatened to redirect sugar cane to ethanol production. Higher-than-expected production levels in Thailand will further add to a global sugar surplus, Rabobank analysts said.

Front-month sugar contracts rose 1.5% to 14 cents to Monday as oil moved back above $100 Monday. Prices were below the 15.9 cents peak reached last month.

Write to Joe Stonor at josephmichael.stonor@wsj.com

data.title: Agape ATP 10-K: Revenue $1.52M; Net loss $2.31M
data.description:
Agape ATP reported total revenue of $1.524 million for the year, an increase of 15.2% year-over-year, while the company recorded a net loss of $2.308 million, an improvement versus the prior year. Gross profit was approximately $0.838 million, representing a roughly 55.0% gross margin down from 57.4% the prior year. The filing highlights a strategic operational shift toward wellness and green energy initiatives that drove the revenue growth.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $1,524,262 — total revenue increased 15.2% versus the prior year.
  • Gross Profit: $838,000 — gross margin approximately 55.0% (down from 57.4% prior year).
  • Net Income: $(2,308,000) — net loss improved ~7.2% versus prior year (prior year net loss $2,486,044).

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Growth Drivers: Revenue growth was driven by new streams in wellness and green energy operations.
  • Channel Shift: The company executed a strategic shift away from network marketing toward a wellness lifestyle and green energy focus, reducing network sales by approximately 48%.
  • Wellness Momentum: A digital advertising and marketing push propelled wellness product revenue, with reported growth of about 949%.
  • Green Energy Launch: Agape ATP launched green energy operations, achieving significant revenue growth in that line (226.7%) despite low gross margins.

Original SEC Filing: Agape ATP Corp [ ATPC ] - 10-K - Apr. 13, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: EU should reconsider its plans to ban imports of Russian gas, Eni CEO says
data.description:
The European Union should reconsider its plans to progressively ban imports of Russian gas from the start of next year, the head of Italian energy group Eni says.

Russia faces a European ban on imports of Russian liquefied natural gas on short-term contracts from April 25 and on long-term contracts from January 1, 2027.

However, Eni CEO Claudio Descalzi was quoted by Italian news agencies as saying it was unclear how the bloc could replace 20 billion cubic metres of Russian LNG from Russia.

The Iran war had disrupted energy shipments in what he called "the most important event of the last 40 years" to affect energy supplies. He made the comments at an event organised by Italy's co-ruling League party on Sunday.

The U.S.-Israeli war against Iran has shut the Strait of Hormuz - a waterway that normally carries about a fifth of global LNG supplies - to most ships, resulting in a tighter supply outlook and higher gas prices.

Last week, the European Network of Transmission System Operators for Gas (Entsog) issued a warning about gas storage levels, saying the conflict in the Middle East is restricting Europe's ability to refill storages, which at the beginning of this month were at their lowest level since 2022.

data.title: Canada Building Permits Drop 8.4% in February
data.description:
By Robb M. Stewart

OTTAWA--Canadian building permits were down sharply in February as a slump in nonresidential-construction plans weighed heavily.

The total value of building permits dropped 8.4% from the month before to a seasonally adjusted 12.09 billion Canadian dollars, the equivalent of US$8.74 billion, Statistics Canada said Monday. Compared with a year earlier, building permits were down 8.6%.

The retreat for the month erodes a downwardly revised 3.5% increase in the value of permits in the first month of this year, adding to recent signs of weakness in the country's property market.

Statistics Canada said construction intentions for nonresidential buildings sank 24% from the period month to C$3.96 billion. Permits for institutional construction accounted for most of the decline, and most of the fall was in the province of Ontario, with weakness also noted in Alberta and Quebec.

Commercial permits were down for a fourth consecutive month, again led by Ontario, with declines also recorded in Nova Scotia and Alberta.

The residential sector was firmer, with the value of permits up 1.7% to C$8.13 billion, the data agency said. That builds on a 1.4% month-over-month rise in January.

Intentions to build multifamily dwellings increased 3.4% for the month, though plans for single-family homes were down 1.6%.

Building permits provide an early indication of construction activity in Canada and are based on a survey of 2,400 municipalities, representing 95% of the country's population. The issuance of a permit doesn't guarantee that construction is imminent.

Canada Mortgage and Housing Corp. has forecast housing demand will gain momentum this year, though sales are projected to stay below historical averages and prices will likely show only modest gains after falling in 2025. The national housing agency also expects new-home construction will decline through 2028 as developers face high costs, weaker demand and more unsold homes, with condominium starts set to be especially weak.

The agency's most recent data showed housing starts rose 4.5% between January and February, though on a six-month moving average the trend was almost flat, edging up 0.4%.

Write to Robb M. Stewart at robb.stewart@wsj.com

data.title: Oil price spike due to Iran war kept Israel rates on hold last month, minutes show
data.description:
By Steven Scheer

Higher energy prices stemming from the conflict with Iran and concerns this will fuel inflation led Israeli policymakers to hold interest rates steady on March 30 for the second straight month, minutes of the discussions showed on Monday.

At the policy meeting, all four members of the Bank of Israel's Monetary Policy Committee voted to leave the short-term interest rate unchanged at 4%.

The MPC had cut the key rate (ILINR=ECI) in November and again in January before standing pat the last two meetings due to inflationary concerns stemming from the U.S.-Israel conflict with Iran.

The U.S. and Israel began attacking Iran on February 28. Iran all but shut the Strait of Hormuz and oil prices jumped in response. The fighting stopped on April 8, but the ceasefire remains fragile.

MPC members noted that while the annual inflation rate edged up to 2.0% in February - within an official 1-3% annual target range - the data did not reflect the rise in energy prices. "These will be reflected in the coming months," the minutes said.

As a result, the central bank sees inflation at a 2.3% rate in 2026, up from a previous estimate in January of 2.0%.

"The Committee’s assessment is that there are increased risks of a renewed increase in inflation — geopolitical developments and their impact on economic activity and on energy prices, increased demand alongside supply constraints, and fiscal developments," the bank said, also citing labour supply constraints.

As such, the bank and forecasters now see perhaps one rate cut in 2026, versus as many as four prior to the Iran air war.

Bank of Israel Governor Amir Yaron has not ruled out rate reductions this year depending on inflation developments.

Economic growth is also projected to be far smaller this year than previously expected - 3.8% versus 5.2%.

One bright spot for inflation has been a stronger shekel versus the dollar, reaching a 1995 peak last week along with record high share prices in Tel Aviv.

data.title: Goldman Sachs’ M&A Boom, Trading Strength Lift Q1 Revenue – But Higher Credit Costs Spook Retail Investors
data.description:
Key points:
  • First-quarter net revenues climbed 14% to $17.23 billion while earnings rose to $17.55 per share from $14.12.
  • Global Banking & Markets revenue rose 19% to $12.74 billion, driven by a 48% surge in investment banking fees on strong M&A activity.
  • Provision for credit losses increased 10% to $315 million, primarily due to impairments related to wholesale loans.

Goldman Sachs (GS) delivered a strong first-quarter performance, with net revenues rising 14% year over year to $17.23 billion, driven by solid growth in its Global Banking & Markets unit and strength in equities financing.

Diluted earnings per share (EPS) for the first quarter (Q1) rose to $17.55 from $14.12 a year earlier, with both revenue and profit topping Wall Street expectations, according to Fiscal.ai data.

Meanwhile, Chairman and CEO David Solomon highlighted the bank’s strong performance despite a volatile market, stressing the importance of disciplined risk management amid a “very complex geopolitical landscape.”

Despite the beat, GS shares were down more than 4% in pre-market trading on Monday.

M&A Activities Drive Core Segment

Revenue in its core segment, Global Banking & Markets, climbed 19% to $12.74 billion, supported by a sharp 48% jump in investment banking fees amid a surge in mergers and acquisitions (M&A) activities.

Equities revenue came in at $5.33 billion, marking a 27% increase, fueled by strong gains in financing, especially prime services, and improved trading performance, with higher intermediation revenues led by gains in cash equity products.

Fixed Income, Currency and Commodities (FICC) net revenues fell 10% to $4.01 billion, mainly due to weaker trading in interest rate products, mortgages, and credit. This decline was partly offset by stronger performance in commodities and currencies.

Provision for credit losses increased 10% to $315 million, primarily due to growth and impairments related to wholesale loans. Goldman Sachs had declared a dividend of $4.50 per share on Friday. The dividend will be paid out on June 29, 2026.

How Did Retail Traders React?

Despite the pre-market slide, retail sentiment on Stocktwits turned ‘bullish’ from ‘neutral’ a day earlier, amid ‘high’ message volumes.

One user said the bank should have capitalized more on volatility.

https://stocktwits.com/skiddy5/message/650094835

Another user called the higher provisions a “bad sign.”

https://stocktwits.com/ycdctx/message/650096899

GS shares have gained more than 85% over the past year.

data.title: Azerbaijan's January-March oil and condensate output at 6.6 million tons, energy ministry says
data.description:
Azerbaijan’s oil and condensate production in January-March fell to 6.6 million metric tons from 6.9 million tons in the same period a year earlier, the energy ministry said on Monday.

Azerbaijan’s gas production was unchanged at 12.6 billion cubic metres (bcm) in January–March 2026, while oil exports fell to 5.2 million tonnes from 5.7 million tonnes, it said.

Britain’s BP reduced its oil production in Azerbaijan to 3.9 million tonnes in January-March 2026 from 4.0 million tonnes in the same period a year earlier.

Gas exports to Europe were unchanged at 3.0 bcm. Overall gas exports rose to 6.5 bcm from 6.0 bcm, the ministry said.

Gas exports to Turkey increased to 2.4 bcm from 2.3 bcm, while exports to Georgia rose to 0.8 bcm from 0.7 bcm. Gas deliveries to Turkey via the TANAP pipeline increased to 1.5 bcm from 1.4 bcm.

Azerbaijan exported 0.3 bcm of gas to Syria, the ministry said.

Azerbaijan’s gas production was unchanged at 12.6 billion cubic metres in January–March 2026.

data.title: South African rand falls as US-Iran tensions lift oil and dent risk appetite
data.description:
South Africa's rand fell on Monday after peace talks between the U.S. and Iran over the weekend broke down, lifting oil prices back above $100 a barrel and denting global risk appetite.

  • At 1228 GMT the rand traded at 16.58 against the dollar , about 0.9% weaker than its previous close.

  • The United States announced over the weekend that it would enforce a naval blockade on all shipping entering or leaving Iranian ports and coastal waters in the Arabian Gulf and Gulf of Oman from Monday, following the collapse of direct talks in Islamabad.

  • Global oil prices surged back above $100 a barrel, hurting emerging market currencies, especially those of net oil importers like South Africa and India .

  • ETM Analytics said that, for South Africa, already grappling with high fuel costs and energy security concerns, the crisis threatens to raise import costs, disrupt tanker routes previously protected by its friendly status, and intensify domestic inflationary pressures.

  • "The (rand) will come under pressure, and South Africa's alignment with Iran appears increasingly unhelpful. One could extend this argument to the broader BRICS+ bloc, which has offered limited support to Iran and is unlikely to provide meaningful backing to South Africa," ETM Analytics said in a research note.

  • On the Johannesburg Stock Exchange, the Top-40 index was down 1%.

  • South Africa's benchmark 2035 government bond (ZAR2035=) weakened, as the yield rose 17.5 basis points to 8.575%.

data.title: Wall St set for subdued open as failed US-Iran peace talks fuel investor angst
data.description:
Key points:
  • Futures down: Dow 1.06%, S&P 500 0.63%, Nasdaq 0.62%
  • Goldman Sachs drops after earnings
  • Sandisk rises on Nasdaq-100 inclusion

By Niket Nishant and Avinash P

Wall Street's main indexes were set to open lower on Monday after weekend talks between the U.S. and Iran failed to deliver a deal to end the war, threatening a nascent recovery in equities and risking a renewed bout of volatility.

The declines indicate that any relief from the ceasefire reached last week could be fleeting, underscoring the risks of leaning too heavily into bullish bets when the geopolitical environment remains uncertain.

The main U.S. stock indexes had logged their second consecutive week of gains on Friday on hopes that the peace talks in Pakistan would bear fruit.

On Monday, however, Dow E-minis (YMcv1) fell 509 points, or 1.06%, as of 08:32 a.m. ET, S&P 500 E-minis dropped 43.25 points, or 0.63%, and Nasdaq 100 E-minis slipped 157.5 points, or 0.62%.

Adding to the unease, the U.S. military is hours away from beginning a blockade of all maritime traffic entering or leaving Iranian ports and coastal areas, in a move aimed at ramping up pressure on Tehran.

The CBOE Market Volatility Index , the market's fear gauge, climbed to 21.29 points.

The shift in sentiment was also visible across other asset classes, with investors gravitating toward the safe-haven U.S. dollar while trimming exposure to equities across geographies.

Oil prices jumped back above $100 a barrel, aggravating inflation worries after data last week showed that a record surge in the cost of gasoline and diesel prompted the biggest increase in U.S. consumer prices in nearly four years in March.

"We are back in the realm of a wide range of plausible outcomes, from another round of negotiations during a patchy ceasefire... to a full resumption of hostilities," said Hasnain Malik, Tellimer's geopolitical risk and EM equity strategist.

GOLDMAN DROPS AFTER EARNINGS

Hopes for relief on the earnings front were dashed after Goldman Sachs shares fell 4.5% in premarket trading, after the bank's quarterly results.

While the investment bank beat estimates for earnings per share, it did so by the slimmest margin in nearly two years.

"We don't see the market really paying too much attention to the earnings beat. And it's all because of prospects of higher inflation, weaker economic activity and a Fed that may be forced to stay on hold for a long, long time," said Peter Cardillo, chief market economist at Spartan Capital Securities.

Attention now turns to commentary from Goldman executives, which will be parsed for clues on how the Middle East conflict, now in its seventh week, is impacting the economy and capital markets.

Rivals Morgan Stanley , JPMorgan Chase and Citigroup slipped 2%, 1.9% and 1.8%, respectively.

Industrial supplies distributor Fastenal's shares also fell 3% after earnings.

Travel-related stocks dropped, with carriers such as Delta Air Lines and JetBlue Airways down 2.7% and 2.3%, respectively, on concerns that higher oil prices might push up fuel costs.

Energy stocks gained, with Chevron , Exxon Mobil and ConocoPhillips climbing 1.9%, 2% and 2.3%, respectively.

Sandisk added 1.5% premarket as the memory chipmaker was on track to join the Nasdaq-100 index on April 20.

Later in the day, data is due on existing home sales in the U.S., and Federal Reserve Governor Stephen Miran is scheduled to speak.

data.title: Nokia to Benefit From Continued Hyperscaler Demand — Market Talk
data.description: Nokia could raise guidance at its optical networks business as data-center spending continues to drive demand, Bank of America Securities analysts write. Following its former mobile phone dominance and transition to telecom equipment, Nokia is now transforming into an optical networks powerhouse with a European advantage, the bank says. "We believe Nokia has multiple levers as it rides growing momentum in the optical industry, a key bottleneck in the global AI data center buildout." Nokia's mobile infrastructure unit is set to improve margins via cost efficiencies and software focus with further upside potential from Huawei/ZTE replacement in Europe and its Nvidia partnership. BofA upgrades Nokia to buy from neutral and lifts its price objective to 10.70 euros from 6.87 euros. Shares rise 1.6% to 8.16 euros. (dominic.chopping@wsj.com)

data.title: Goldman Sachs Earnings Climb 19% as Bankers, Stock Traders Notch Wins. Stock Tumbles. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Rebecca Ungarino

Goldman Sachs' quarterly earnings jumped 19% from a year ago on robust investment-banking fees and record revenue in equities.

The New York-based firm reported first-quarter net earnings of $5.63 billion, which amounted to per-share earnings of $17.55. Net revenue was $17.23 billion, topping analysts' estimates for earnings of $16.47 a share and revenue of $17 billion, according to FactSet.

This is a developing story. Please check back for updates.

Write to Rebecca Ungarino at rebecca.ungarino@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Bill Ackman Begins Marketing Pershing Square IPO, Touts ‘Extraordinary Economic Characteristics’ As He Looks To Raise Up To $10B
data.description:
Key points:
  • Pershing Square stated in its filing that the offering will take a dual listing approach.
  • This includes the shares of Pershing Square USA, a closed-end fund, as well as Pershing Square Inc., the hedge fund and the prospective parent company of Pershing Square Capital Management.
  • For every 100 shares of the closed-end fund subscribed by investors, they will also receive 20 shares of the hedge fund.

Bill Ackman has kicked off marketing for the initial public offering of his hedge fund and closed-end fund, adopting a dual-listing approach and expecting to raise between $5 billion and $10 billion.

The combined IPO will result in two companies being taken public. This includes Pershing Square Inc. (PS), the prospective parent company of Pershing Square Capital Management, and a closed-end fund, Pershing Square USA (PSUS).

In its filing with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC), Pershing Square stated that it has already secured $2.8 billion in commitments ahead of the IPO.

Ackman Touts ‘Extraordinary Economic Characteristics’

In a letter to shareholders, the billionaire investor touted “extraordinary economic characteristics” of Pershing Square as he sought to explain the fund’s long-term goal.

“Our long-term goal for Pershing Square Inc. is to build one of the most valuable companies in the world by generating one of the best long-term performance records of any investor ever,” Ackman said.

He added that, with permanent capital, Pershing Square has the ideal structure to pursue its investment strategy, which involves acquiring and long-term owning what the company considers some of the greatest businesses and long-term compounders of shareholder value.

Ackman also explained that PSI can be considered a “meta” company, earning long-term fees from funds invested in high-quality businesses. He said that Pershing Square’s focus on liquid, large-cap stocks allows it to adjust positions easily and stay invested without pressure from investor flows.

What Will Investors Get?

Investors who subscribe to shares of Pershing Square USA will also receive shares of the hedge fund, Pershing Square Inc. According to the SEC filing, investors who buy 100 shares of the closed-end fund PSUS will also receive 20 shares of the hedge fund, PSI.

The closed-end fund’s shares are priced at $50 apiece, and the shares they will receive in the hedge fund will come at no extra cost.

Investors participating in the $2.8 billion private placement will receive 30 shares of the hedge fund for every 100 shares of the closed-end fund, according to the filing.

Nearly Two-Year Delay

Ackman had previously sought to take Pershing Square public in 2024, at a valuation of up to $25 billion. However, he shelved the plans, citing “enormous” sensitivity to the transaction size, in a letter to shareholders in July 2024.

Pershing Square has about $30.7 billion in assets under management. The company stated that its closed-end fund, PSUS, will charge a 2% management fee but not a performance fee.

PSHZF stock was flat in Monday’s pre-market trade. The Renaissance IPO ETF (IPO) is up 26% over the past 12 months.

data.title: U.S. Futures Tumble as Iran Blockade Move Sparks Fresh Geopolitical Shock
data.description:
U.S. stock index futures pointed to a lower open Monday after diplomatic discussions between Washington and Tehran failed to produce an agreement.

The development came alongside a directive to begin enforcing a maritime blockade on vessels moving in and out of Iranian ports. The enforcement action was scheduled to take effect at 10 a.m. Eastern Time on April 13, 2026.

Nasdaq 100 futures eased about half a percent in early trading. S&P 500 futures moved down a similar fraction, while Dow futures also edged into negative territory.

The fresh geopolitical friction weighed on sentiment after a weekend that offered little clarity on de-escalation. Traders appeared to trim exposure ahead of the regular session, reflecting caution over potential supply chain or energy market disruptions.

Oil prices climbed in electronic trade as the blockade news raised concerns about crude flows through a critical regional chokepoint. Broader markets were likely to remain sensitive to any further signals from the administration regarding the scope or duration of the naval enforcement.
